decisions.choice: Position v1 as a local-first browser assistant for turning scattered knowledge into reviewed opportunities and shared memory for a community or project
decisions.rationale: The v1 promise should be readable in one line and should not require users to understand agent control planes, onchain rails, or internal graph mechanics.

decisions.choice: Hide advanced rails by default instead of deleting code
decisions.rationale: Store-readiness and production-readiness are green; removing working modules before submission adds release risk while hiding them improves surface clarity.

decisions.choice: Add a persisted simple/advanced UI mode through existing UI preferences
decisions.rationale: The existing Dexie plus chrome.storage sync path already carries browser UI preferences, so this avoids build flags, env sprawl, or a new feature-flag system.

decisions.choice: Default simple mode should expose capture, review, focus, sharing, members, and basic settings
decisions.rationale: A new user should understand Coop as a friendly project/community tool, not as an agent dashboard.

decisions.choice: Keep advanced mode available for trusted operators and builders
decisions.rationale: Autoresearch, sources, proof details, FVM, stealth, policy, permits, and debugging surfaces are useful for builders but should not be part of the default product story.
The popup is already the strongest simple surface; this pass should only hide obvious advanced controls and avoid broad layout churn.
